meta.name: SMFQ Short Mood and Feelings Questionnaire
meta.description: The SMFQ is a 13-item scale measuring depressive symptoms in children and adolescents (aged 6-17).
meta.author: Angold A, et al. (1995)
meta.reference: Angold A, et al. Development of a short questionnaire for use in epidemiological studies of depression in children and adolescents. Int J Methods Psychiatr Res. 1995;5:237-249.
meta.instruction: These sentences describe how people may feel or act. For each item, please select how much you have felt or acted this way in the past two weeks.

questions.text: I didn't enjoy anything at all.
I felt so tired I just sat around and did nothing.
I felt I was no good anymore.
I found it hard to think properly or concentrate.
I thought nobody really loved me.
I thought I could never be as good as other kids.

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, suggesting no significant depressive symptoms. Children and adolescents commonly experience some mood fluctuations, and your current emotional functioning appears healthy.
suggestions: Continue encouraging open communication about feelings
Maintain regular routines including consistent sleep schedules
Support engagement in enjoyable activities and social interactions
Occasional sadness is a normal part of development

ranges.label: Mild Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild depressive symptoms. At this level, a young person may show some changes in mood, energy, or interest in activities. These symptoms are worth monitoring but may not yet significantly impair daily functioning.
suggestions: Monitor mood changes over the coming weeks to see if they persist or worsen
Encourage regular physical activity, social engagement, and consistent sleep
Create a supportive environment where the young person feels safe expressing emotions
Consider speaking with a school counselor or pediatrician if concerns continue
Limit excessive screen time and encourage meaningful activities

ranges.label: Significant Depressive Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates significant depressive symptoms that are likely affecting daily functioning, school performance, and social relationships. At this level, professional evaluation is recommended to determine appropriate support and intervention.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional experienced with youth
Talk to the young person's pediatrician or school counselor about available resources
Maintain a structured, supportive daily routine at home
Evidence-based treatments such as CBT or interpersonal therapy are effective for youth depression
If there are any concerns about self-harm, seek immediate professional help
